source.submodule: Math Operators and overall code structure

topicTags: Math Operators and overall code structure

content.problemStatement:
Let us now look at the syntax of Kotlin and what it means.

- `fun`:
  - In Kotlin, fun is a keyword used to declare a function. It indicates that you are defining a function.
  - In the example in editor, it declares the main() function.
- `main`: 
  - main is the name of the function. In Kotlin, the main function is the entry point of the program. When you run a Kotlin program, the execution starts from this function.
  - Any code inside the main() function's curly brackets {} will be executed.
- `args: Array`: 
  - This part defines the function parameter. args is the name of the parameter, and Array specifies the data type of the parameter.
  - In this case, args is an array of strings, which is used to pass command-line arguments to the program.
  - Don't worry if you didn't understand it, you are going to learn more about parameters in coming modules.
- `{}`: 
  - The curly braces define the body of the main function. Inside these braces, you write the code that will be executed when the main function is called.
  - Any code inside its curly brackets {} will be executed.

- If there are multiple functions, this is the function which is executed first.

**Try displaying the sum of 12 & 7 using the given code**
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
